rulings.Case Title: SANG AU SU, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL VS. NAM MUI AU, AN INDIVIDUAL ALSO KNOWN AS NAM MUOI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2025 line 1. DEFEDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS is GRANTED pursuant to CCP section 581(f)(2). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT CCSF'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to July 31, 2025 on the Court's own motion. rulings.Case Title: ESSEX FOX PLAZA, LP VS. RACHELLE BLACK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession And Monetary Damages Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2025 line 6. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION AND MONETARY DAMAGES PURSUANT TO STIPULATION is DENIED. Plaintiff's ledger reflects that defendant fully complied with her obligations under the Stipulation as of January 23, 2025. The terms of the Stipulation required plaintiff to dismiss the action with prejudice upon defendant's full compliance. To the extent plaintiff seeks judgment based on payment obligations that arose after January 23, 2025, the Stipulation is unenforceable as to those payments. Plaintiff cannot obtain judgment in this action after plaintiff was required to dismiss this action. Plaintiff's supplemental filings which were filed less than 5 court days before the hearing date are stricken as untimely. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AVA NINTH, L.P. VS. LUIS SANTANA-LOZADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Default Judgment And Recall The Writ Of Possession, Or, In The Alternative, Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2025 line 9. DEFENDANTS LUIS SANTANA-LOZADA, ERIC LANDRUM, AND NINA VAN ESS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT, VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND RECALL THE WRIT OF POSSESSION, OR QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is GRANTED in part, based on the mandatory provision of CCP 473(b) only. Plaintiff's untimely opposition is stricken. The defaults of defendants Eric Landrum, Luis Santana-Lozada, Nina Van Ess are set aside. The default judgment entered on May 20, 2025 is vacated. The writ issued on June 5, 2025 is recalled. Defendants' alternative request to quash service of summons is DENIED. Defendants shall file and serve the proposed response within five days. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES, INC. VS. HUIQI TANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2025 line 10. DEFENDANT HUIQI TANG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Defendant does not provide any authority for the implicit proposition that alleging an accurate ZIP code for the premises is an essential element of plaintiff's cause of action for unlawful detainer. The discrepancy between the ZIP codes provided for the premises in different parts of the pleading does not render the pleading so ambiguous and unintelligible that defendant cannot respond. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
